title: Extraction Chromatographic Studies of Zirconium(IV) with High Molecular Weight Carboxylic Acid on Silica Gel
author: DEB K. GHOSH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Visva-Bharati, Santiniketan-731 235 

  Manuscript received 10 April 1990, revised 4 March 1991, accepted 27 May 1991  

 A selective method has been developed for reversed phase extraction chromatographic studies of zireonium(IV) with high molecular weight carboxylic acid, Versatic 10, as a stationary phase on a column of silica gel. Quantitative extraction of zirconium has been achieved from acetic acid solution across the pH range 1,5- 4.8. The effect of variables such as pH, stripping agents and flow rate on extraction and elution have been studied. Exchange capacity of the exchanger has been determined at different temperatures. Zirconium has been separated from a variety of elements.
